summary: I practiced general dentistry in Morehead, Kentucky for over forty years in a private practice. My daughter and I were partners there for about twenty years before she left to work at the University of Kentucky. Morehead was also where I started providing free dental care through a local free clinic and got involved with Remote Area Medical clinics.

I attended the University of Kentucky College of Dentistry, where I received excellent education that prepared me well for my career. My daughter is currently an instructor there and was my partner in Morehead for about twenty years. The university played a key role in my dental training and my family's professional life.

In 2016, I moved to Mount Sterling after living in Morehead for many years. There, I got involved with The Post Clinic, which was then a medical clinic providing free care. I helped start the dental services at The Post Clinic and worked to expand its dental care offerings during my six years living in Mount Sterling.

The Post Clinic started as a medical free clinic, and I became involved around 2016 to help establish dental services there. We began with limited volunteer support and no-fee care for qualifying patients, eventually securing a federal grant to build a new dental clinic with updated equipment. I served as the dental clinic director and helped grow the clinic to provide care one to two days a week, seeing many grateful patients and fostering a caring, personal environment.

About fifteen to twenty years ago, I got involved with Remote Area Medical, a group founded by Stan Brock that provides free care through mobile clinics. I hosted several of their clinics in Morehead and participated in their weekend events. This experience inspired me to focus more on providing free dental care locally in Eastern Kentucky.

In the 1990s, I went on a mission trip to Nicaragua, which was a very eye-opening experience. I saw firsthand the incredible need for dental care as people walked miles daily to receive treatment. This motivated me to focus on providing free care closer to home in Eastern Kentucky rather than traveling abroad.

Stan Brock founded Remote Area Medical, a group I became involved with to provide free dental care. His organization runs three-day clinics across the country, and I participated in some of these events on weekends. His work helped inspire my commitment to free dental care in my community.

A husband and wife dentist team moved here from Charlotte, North Carolina, and they are currently the dentists providing care at The Post Clinic. They have become an important part of the clinic's dental services, working one to two days a week. Their relocation has helped stabilize the clinic’s ability to offer regular dental care.

While Medicaid was not discussed in detail, it is implied that many patients at The Post Clinic qualify for free or reduced-cost care based on income levels, which often relates to Medicaid eligibility. The clinic aims to serve those who cannot afford dental services, many of whom may be Medicaid recipients or uninsured. Funding challenges remain in providing paid dental services at the clinic.

Medicare was not explicitly mentioned in the transcript, but given the patient population and the clinic’s focus on underserved individuals, some patients may be Medicare beneficiaries. The Post Clinic provides care to those who cannot afford dental services, which often includes elderly patients covered by Medicare without dental benefits. The clinic’s mission complements gaps in Medicare dental coverage.

Dr. Tanya Kendrick is one of the dentists currently working at The Post Clinic. She and her husband, both dentists from Charlotte, North Carolina, provide dental care one to two days a week. We pay her by the day, and we hope to secure more funding to support her and expand services in the future.

I currently live in Lexington, Kentucky, which means I commute to Mount Sterling to be involved with The Post Clinic. Lexington is where I reside now after moving from Mount Sterling in recent years. Living in Lexington allows me to stay connected with the clinic while enjoying retirement life.
